findings.claim_text: The share price didn't reclaim its January high until roughly mid-July 2025 — about 5.5 months after the shock — and the proximate catalyst was the Trump administration's reversal of the H20 China-export ban, following direct lobbying from Huang and a broader US-China trade framework deal [^7].
findings.contradicting_quote: Nvidia shares hit an all-time high on Wednesday, June 25 [2025]... shares have jumped more than 14% since Nvidia's May 28 earnings report

findings.claim_text: Nvidia's move was more modest: -2.40% on July 16 (close $207.40) followed by a further -1.04% on July 17 (close $205.25), a cumulative two-day decline of roughly 3.4% [^16].

The first is the "$1 trillion in chipmaker value erased" / "SOX down more than 10% over two sessions" figure that has been recirculating as if it belonged to the Kimi K3 event. It doesn't: that number traces to a Broadcom-earnings-driven chip selloff on June 5, 2026 — six weeks before Moonshot AI shipped anything — and the original reporting makes no mention of Kimi K3 or Moonshot at all [^18].
